votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the character set equality check using Python sets, which matches the Coq specification.
The Python code correctly implements the character set equivalence check using Python's built-in set operations, which perfectly matches the Coq specification.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification using Python's built-in set equality to check if two strings contain the same characters.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. InStr checks character membership, same_char_sets checks if two strings have the same set of characters, and same_chars_spec verifies that res equals the result of same_char_sets. The Coq spec states res=true iff same_char_sets holds and res=false iff it doesn't, which is equivalent to checking res == same_char_sets(s0, s1). For all concrete inputs, both will produce the same judgments.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. InStr checks character membership, same_char_sets checks if two strings have the same set of characters (equivalent to the Coq biconditional for all characters), and same_chars_spec returns true iff res matches whether the strings have the same character sets. The Coq spec's biconditional structure (res=true iff same_char_sets, res=false iff not same_char_sets) is equivalent to checking res == same_char_sets for boolean res.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. InStr checks character membership, same_char_sets checks if two strings have the same set of characters, and same_chars_spec returns true iff res matches whether the strings have the same character sets. The Coq spec's biconditional structure (res=true iff same_char_sets, res=false iff not same_char_sets) is equivalent to checking res == same_char_sets(s0, s1) for boolean res.

InStr matches character membership for single-character inputs, same_char_sets via set equality matches the Coq membership-based definition, and same_chars_spec correctly checks that res equals this predicate.

same_char_sets compares character presence ignoring multiplicity; Python set(s0)==set(s1) matches this, and same_chars_spec returns True iff res matches that truth. InStr also matches for single-character inputs (the Coq ascii case).

InStr checks character membership, same_char_sets compares character sets, and same_chars_spec returns whether res matches that; the Python implementations align with the Coq semantics for all valid (single-character) inputs.
